meta_title: Impact Windows in Sunrise, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Sunrise, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & meet Broward County code. Free estimates!
h1: Impact Windows in Sunrise, FL — Protect Your Home Where the Suburbs Meet the Storm Path
intro:
Sunrise, Florida sits squarely in one of Broward County's most active residential corridors — a community of nearly 100,000 people whose neighborhoods stretch from the bustling retail energy of Sawgrass Mills Mall to the quiet, tree-lined streets of Sunrise Lakes and Welleby. With an average home value of $350,000 and a housing stock where roughly half of all properties were built before 1994, the question isn't whether Sunrise homeowners need impact windows — it's how long they can afford to wait. The 170 mph wind zone designation covering all of ZIP codes 33313, 33321, 33322, 33323, 33325, 33345, and 33351 tells you everything you need to know about the atmospheric forces this city is engineered to withstand.

Unlike communities directly on the Atlantic coastline, Sunrise sits approximately 10 miles inland — a detail that sometimes leads homeowners to underestimate their exposure. That's a costly misconception. Inland communities across Broward County experienced some of the most severe structural damage during Hurricane Wilma in 2005 and Hurricane Irma in 2017, precisely because wind-driven debris travels far from the shoreline before it makes contact with windows, garage doors, and entryways. The Sawgrass and Spring Lake neighborhoods, for example, saw widespread damage to older single-family homes with standard aluminum windows that simply weren't rated for the sustained winds and pressure changes these storms delivered. Impact-resistant windows are not a luxury upgrade in Sunrise — they are a fundamental layer of home protection.

Beyond storm safety, impact windows installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing deliver tangible, year-round benefits to Sunrise homeowners. The city's inland location means summer heat is intense and unrelenting, making energy efficiency a genuine financial concern. Modern impact windows with Low-E coatings and multi-chamber frames dramatically reduce solar heat gain, keeping interior temperatures stable and trimming monthly utility bills. Add the noise reduction benefits — particularly relevant for homes near the I-595 corridor, the Nob Hill Road retail strip, or the BB&T Center event traffic — and the value proposition for upgrading to impact windows becomes clear from every angle.

section_why.h2: Why Sunrise Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for the Job
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has earned the title 'The Kings of Service' by doing something most window companies in South Florida don't: keeping every single installation in-house. With 60+ years of combined experience across our installation teams and more than 500 completed homes throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we've built a reputation on reliability, technical precision, and the kind of follow-through that third-party subcontractors simply cannot guarantee. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the same team that quotes your project is the team that shows up at your Sunrise home with the tools, training, and accountability to get it done right.

For Sunrise specifically, our familiarity with the City of Sunrise Building Division permitting process is a significant advantage. We handle the permit application, the inspections, and the final sign-off from start to finish. Homeowners in the Sawgrass and Welleby communities often tell us that their previous experiences with window companies involved lengthy permit delays, surprise fees, or installations that failed inspection — situations that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing eliminates through careful pre-installation planning and strict adherence to Florida Building Code requirements. We know what the Sunrise Building Division inspectors are looking for, and we build that knowledge into every project from day one.

Our product selection reflects both the technical requirements of Broward County's 170 mph wind zone and the aesthetic preferences of Sunrise's diverse homeowner base. We install ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact windows, two of the most respected brands in the South Florida market, available in a wide range of frame profiles, glass configurations, and finishes. Whether you own a 1980s ranch home in Sunrise Lakes or a newer two-story in the Spring Lake subdivision, we'll recommend the right product for your architecture, your budget, and your long-term goals.

Finally, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's A+ BBB Rating and fully licensed and insured status aren't just credentials on a website — they're assurances that you're working with a company that stands behind its work. We offer financing starting from just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it practical for middle-income Sunrise families to invest in whole-home impact window protection without disrupting household finances. From the first consultation to the final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers a seamless, professional experience that reflects the standard our name demands.

section_history.h2: Sunrise Has a Storm History That Demands Better Windows
section_history.body:
Sunrise has been tested by nine named storms since 1990, and three of those events left permanent marks on how this community thinks about home protection. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — the storm that fundamentally rewrote Florida's building code — struck south of Sunrise but sent shockwaves through the entire tri-county region. The catastrophic destruction in Miami-Dade County was a wake-up call for Broward homeowners, yet decades later, a significant portion of Sunrise's pre-1994 housing stock still carries standard windows that predate the modern impact-resistant standards Andrew inspired.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 was arguably Sunrise's most consequential storm experience. Wilma made landfall near Cape Romano on Florida's southwest coast but tracked northeast across the state at unusually high speed, delivering sustained Category 3 winds to Broward County with very little warning time. The rapid onset left many residents without adequate preparation, and homes with standard single-pane or older aluminum-framed windows suffered broken glass, water intrusion, and interior damage across neighborhoods like Nob Hill and Sunrise Lakes. Insurance claims filed by Broward County homeowners after Wilma ran into the billions, reshaping what local carriers would cover and at what premium.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the same lessons for a newer generation of Sunrise homeowners. Though Irma's core tracked up the state's west coast, its outer bands produced damaging winds across all of Broward County, and the pressure differentials alone were enough to flex and crack older window systems. The pattern across each of these storms is consistent: homes with properly installed, code-compliant impact windows sustained dramatically less damage than those relying on shutters, plywood, or aging standard glass. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installations are built to perform where it counts most — when the next storm makes its approach toward South Florida.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code, Broward County Wind Zones, and What Sunrise Requires
section_code.body:
Sunrise falls under Florida's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone adjacency requirements — and while the city itself is not technically classified as HVHZ (that designation applies to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas), the Florida Building Code still mandates that all impact window and door products installed in Sunrise meet stringent hurricane-rated performance standards. Specifically, all window products must carry a valid Florida Product Approval (NOA or FL number) demonstrating compliance with the ASCE 7 wind load standard for a 170 mph design wind speed. This is not a suggestion — it is a legal requirement enforced through the permitting process.

Every impact window installation in Sunrise requires a permit pulled through the City of Sunrise Building Division, located at 10770 W. Oakland Park Blvd. The permit process involves submission of product approval documentation, installation drawings, and contractor licensing credentials before work begins. After installation, a city inspector must verify that the windows were installed in accordance with the manufacturer's approved installation instructions and the applicable sections of the Florida Building Code, Chapter 14 (Exterior Walls) and the referenced ASTM standards for glazing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process on behalf of our clients — no homeowner should have to navigate building department bureaucracy alone.

For homes built before 1994 in Sunrise — which represents approximately half of the city's residential housing stock — there are additional considerations. Older rough openings may require modification to accept modern impact window frames, and the existing wall construction must be evaluated to ensure the new anchoring system can achieve the required fastener pull-out values. Our installation teams perform a thorough pre-installation assessment of every opening to identify and address these conditions before they become problems. This level of detail is what separates a compliant, long-lasting installation from one that passes inspection today but fails structurally when a real storm arrives.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI: The Impact Window Brands Built for Broward County
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium impact window lines in Sunrise: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI, both of which carry Florida Product Approvals and are engineered to perform in the demanding climate conditions of South Florida's inland Broward County corridor. These are not off-the-shelf products shipped from out-of-state warehouses — they are purpose-built impact systems tested and certified specifically for the wind loads, humidity cycles, and UV exposure that Sunrise homes experience year after year.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) are particularly well regarded for their laminated glass construction, which bonds two panes of glass to a durable interlayer that prevents shattering upon impact. Even when the outer glass surface is struck by wind-borne debris, the interlayer holds the glass in place, maintaining the window's structural integrity and preventing breach of the building envelope. ESW frames feature multi-chamber profiles that add rigidity while minimizing thermal bridging — an important consideration for Sunrise homeowners looking to reduce the solar heat gain that drives up air conditioning costs throughout the long Florida summer. Low-E coating options further enhance energy performance by reflecting infrared radiation before it enters the home.

CWI impact windows complement ESW with a robust product line that includes single-hung, double-hung, casement, sliding, and picture configurations — giving Sunrise homeowners maximum flexibility to match their existing window layout or redesign openings for improved ventilation and aesthetics. Both brands offer multiple frame color and finish options suited to the architectural styles common in Sunrise's neighborhoods, from the Mediterranean-influenced exteriors near Sawgrass to the more contemporary profiles found in newer Nob Hill developments. Our team will help you select the right product combination for your home's specific requirements, ensuring both compliance and curb appeal.

section_cost.h2: Impact Window Costs in Sunrise: What to Expect and How to Finance Your Investment
section_cost.body:
For a Sunrise home with an average value of $350,000, a full impact window installation typically represents an investment between $8,000 and $20,000 depending on the number of openings, the window configurations selected, and the complexity of the installation. Single-hung impact windows in standard sizes are the most economical option, while casement or custom-shaped windows in larger openings will carry a higher price per unit. The good news is that this investment comes with measurable financial returns that begin accruing from the moment installation is complete — through energy savings, insurance premium reductions, and increased resale value.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed to make whole-home impact window replacement accessible for middle-income Sunrise families. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month with $0 down required, meaning you don't need to deplete savings or wait years to accumulate the full project cost before getting protected. Spreading the investment over a manageable monthly payment while immediately benefiting from lower utility bills and reduced insurance premiums makes the financial math work in your favor from day one. We work with multiple lending partners to find the right terms for your household budget.

The return on investment for impact windows in Sunrise is well-documented. Energy savings from Low-E impact glass can reduce cooling costs by 10–25% annually in a South Florida home — meaningful savings given the region's year-round air conditioning demand. Insurance premium discounts for qualifying impact window installations in Broward County frequently range from 15–45% of the wind premium component, which on a $350,000 home can translate to hundreds of dollars per year. When you factor in the increased resale appeal — buyers in South Florida actively seek homes with full impact protection already installed — the total return on a properly executed impact window project consistently outpaces the initial cost.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Windows Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance in Sunrise
section_insurance.body:
Florida's homeowner's insurance market is one of the most volatile in the country, and Broward County residents have felt that volatility acutely over the past decade. Carriers including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others serving the Sunrise market all factor wind mitigation measures into their premium calculations — and impact windows are among the most recognized and rewarded mitigation upgrades available. Under Florida's wind mitigation discount framework, homes with impact-resistant openings on all windows and doors can qualify for significant reductions in the wind portion of their premium, which often constitutes 40–60% of total homeowner's insurance cost in Broward County.

The process for capturing these savings begins with a wind mitigation inspection, typically performed by a licensed home inspector or engineer. The inspector documents the opening protection — verifying that all windows and exterior doors are protected by rated impact products — and submits a standardized OIR-B1-1802 wind mitigation form to the homeowner's insurance carrier. Carriers are then required by Florida law to apply the applicable discounts, which vary by carrier and policy structure but can realistically save a Sunrise homeowner $500–$1,500 or more per year depending on the home's insured replacement value and the carrier's rating model.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary product documentation, installation permits, and Florida Product Approval records to support your wind mitigation inspection. Our installations are performed to the precise standards that inspectors verify — anchoring, glazing, and frame specifications are documented at every step. Many of our Sunrise clients report that the annual insurance savings alone cover a substantial portion of their monthly financing payment, effectively allowing the windows to pay for themselves over time. If your current insurance carrier has recently non-renewed your policy or increased your premium significantly — a reality for many Broward County homeowners in recent years — a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing window installation may be one of the most effective steps you can take to stabilize your coverage and reduce your long-term cost of homeownership.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows if I'm 10 miles inland in Sunrise?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Being inland does not significantly reduce your wind exposure in a major hurricane — storms like Wilma and Irma proved that Broward County communities far from the coast can experience Category 2 and Category 3 wind speeds. Additionally, Florida Building Code requires 170 mph-rated products in Sunrise regardless of coastal proximity. Wind-borne debris from commercial areas like Sawgrass Mills and surrounding retail corridors poses a serious threat even in tropical storm conditions. Impact windows protect your home and family whether the storm makes landfall nearby or tracks across the state.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Sunrise home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most Sunrise single-family home projects range from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the number and size of openings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down required, so you can protect your home immediately without a large upfront payment. We work with multiple lending partners to match you with the right program for your budget. Combined with insurance savings and energy cost reductions, many homeowners find the net monthly cost is lower than they expected.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in Sunrise, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle them?
faqs.a: Yes — a building permit from the City of Sunrise Building Division is required for all impact window installations, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire process on your behalf. We submit the permit application, provide Florida Product Approval documentation for our ESW and CWI windows, and coordinate the required inspection with the city. You will receive a fully permitted and code-compliant installation without having to navigate the building department yourself. This is included as part of our standard service, with no hidden permit-processing fees.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation take in Sunrise?
faqs.a: The physical installation for a typical Sunrise single-family home is completed in one to three days depending on the number of openings and any pre-existing conditions that require attention. Permit processing through the City of Sunrise Building Division typically adds two to four weeks to the overall project timeline before work can begin. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing submits permits promptly and keeps you informed at every stage. We schedule installations efficiently to minimize disruption to your household and complete the city inspection as quickly as the Building Division's calendar allows.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance by installing impact windows in Sunrise?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and policy, but Broward County homeowners with full impact window protection commonly see annual wind premium discounts ranging from 15% to 45%. On a $350,000 home in Sunrise, this can translate to $500–$1,500 or more in annual savings depending on your insurer and coverage structure. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your upgrades on the state-required OIR-B1-1802 form, which you submit to your carrier to trigger the discounts. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all product documentation needed to support this process.
